intitule: Exercise 1: Matching Phrasal Verbs to Definitions (Ressource)

enonce.texte:
Instruction: Match each phrasal verb on the left with its correct definition on the right. Write the letter of the correct definition next to the number of the phrasal verb.

List A (Phrasal Verbs):
1. give up
2. put off
3. turn on
4. look for
5. fall through

List B (Definitions):
A. to search
B. to stop trying or quit
C. to postpone or delay
D. to start a machine or light
E. to fail or not happen as planned

questions.texte: Identify the correct match for each phrasal verb.

intitule: Exercise 2: Fill in the Blanks with Phrasal Verbs (Ressource)

enonce.texte:
Instruction: Complete each sentence with the correct phrasal verb from the box. Use the correct tense if necessary.

Box: give up, put off, turn on, look for, fall through, keep at

questions.texte: The electricity was off, so I had to _______ the generator.
Despite the difficulties, the athlete refused to _______.
Our plans to go to the beach _______ because of the heavy rain.
If you want to improve your English, you must _______ practicing every day.

enonce.texte: Context: You are a young entrepreneur in Douala. You have started a small business collecting and recycling plastic bottles (see image). Read the situations below and answer the questions using appropriate phrasal verbs and expressions.
images: A young Cameroonian standing next to a large pile of plastic bottles in an urban neighborhood in Douala, holding a recycling bag, looking determined and hopeful.

questions.texte: After three months, your business is not making a profit. Some friends tell you to stop. How would you explain that you will not stop trying? Use a phrasal verb.
questions.reponse: I will not give up. / I am not going to give up on my business.

questions.texte: You planned a big cleanup event for Saturday, but the local council announces a water shortage for that day. You decide to move the event to Sunday. Which phrasal verb describes this action? Write a full sentence.
questions.reponse: I will put off the event until Sunday. / I am putting off the cleanup because of the water shortage.

questions.texte: A new law requires all businesses to separate organic and non-perishable waste. Your neighbor does not know the difference. Explain to him the meaning of 'non-perishable' using a complete sentence.
questions.reponse: Non-perishable items are things like plastic bottles and cans that do not decay or rot easily.

enonce.texte: Instruction: Complete the dialogues with the correct expression: for a while or of course.

questions.texte: A: 'Can you help me carry these bottles to the recycling center?' B: '_______ . It's my pleasure.'
A: 'Where have you been?' B: 'I was resting at home _______ because I had a fever.'
_______ , I didn't understand the difference between 'phrasal verbs' and 'normal verbs,' but now I do.

enonce.texte:
Instruction: Write a short paragraph (5-7 sentences) about a problem you faced in your community (e.g., waste management, flooding, or bullying at school). Use at least three different phrasal verbs (give up, put off, look for, fall through, turn on, keep at) and one common expression (for a while, of course).

Topic suggestion: How you tried to organize a group to clean a polluted drainage channel in your neighborhood.
images: A group of Cameroonian students in school uniforms working together to remove trash from a blocked drainage channel in front of their school.

questions.reponse: Sample answer: Last month, I decided to look for volunteers to clean the blocked drainage behind our school. The smell was terrible, and many people wanted to put off the work until later. At first, some students gave up because the work was hard. However, I told everyone to keep at it because flooding would cause disease. For a while, we thought our plan might fall through due to a lack of tools. Of course, our teacher helped us turn on the water pump to wash away the mud. Finally, we succeeded!
